memoryArtifacts.headline: Kilchurn Castle was built in the mid-1400s, and it remained the base of the mighty Campbells of Glenorchy for 150 years. After the first Jacobite Rising of 1689, Kilchurn was converted into a garrison stronghold, but was abandoned by the end of the 1700s.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow於1258年1月出生在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾時，他的父親Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell28歲，母親Janet Isabel Sinclair26歲。他於1279年與Alyse Margaret Crawford在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1315年2月在他的家鄉享年57歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow于1258年1月出生在英国苏格兰阿吉爾时，他的父亲Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell28岁，母亲Janet Isabel Sinclair26岁。他于1279年与Alyse Margaret Crawford在英国苏格兰阿吉爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1315年2月在他的家乡享年57岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿吉爾洛霍。

nameMeanings.name: Campbell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Campell
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: variant of   Campbell   (see also   Camel  ).

variants.name: Beauchamp
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Camel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Napoleon, Patrice.

English (of Norman origin) and French: nickname from Norman French came(i)l ‘camel’ (from Latin camelus, classical Greek kamēlos), possibly denoting a clumsy person or someone with long legs. It may also be a topographic or habitational name referring to a house with a sign depicting a camel (although surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English); compare 5 below.
English: possibly a habitational name from Queen Camel and West Camel in Somerset, early recorded as Camel(le), possibly a Celtic name from canto- ‘border, district’ and mēl ‘bare hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Neil
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

variants.name: Neale
meanings: Anglicized form of the enduringly popular Gaelic name   Niall .  From the Middle Ages onwards, this name was found mainly in Ireland, the Highlands of Scotland, and the English-Scottish Border region. However, since the 20th century it has spread to enjoy considerable popularity in all parts of the English-speaking world, although in England it has recently been overtaken by the Gaelic form.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow was born in January 1258, in Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell, was 28 and his mother, Janet Isabel Sinclair, was 26. He married Alyse Margaret Crawford in 1279, in Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in February 1315, in his hometown, at the age of 57, and was buried in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow est né en janvier 1258, à Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Janet Isabel Sinclair, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Alyse Margaret Crawford en 1279, à Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en février 1315, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterré à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow(이)가 1258년 1월 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell(은)는 28세였고, 그의 어머니 Janet Isabel Sinclair(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1279년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이에서 Alyse Margaret Crawford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1315년 2월 그의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 럭아베에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Neil Campbell of Lochow родился январь 1258 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Cailean or Colin Mor Campbell было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Janet Isabel Sinclair, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Alyse Margaret Crawford в 1279 г. в местности аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер февраль 1315 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 57 лет и был похоронен в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия.
